evals.expected_output: Analysis covering: (1) volatile qualifier — correct use for ISR-shared buffer; (2) SPI RXNE interrupt — identifies this as STM32-style CMSIS register access; (3) Ring buffer without critical section — race condition risk if rx_idx is accessed from main context without disabling interrupts; (4) No bounds check on rx_idx before write — though reset to 0 is present, a concurrent read race could corrupt data. Recommendations include memory barriers or disabling interrupts in main context.

assertions.text: Identifies the volatile qualifier as correct for ISR-shared memory
Identifies the race condition risk on rx_idx if accessed from main context
Recognizes STM32/CMSIS register access pattern (SPI1->SR, SPI_SR_RXNE)

evals.prompt:
This Raspberry Pi script reads a temperature sensor over I2C:
```python
import smbus2
bus = smbus2.SMBus(1)
data = bus.read_i2c_block_data(0x48, 0x00, 2)
temp = (data[0] << 4) | (data[1] >> 4)
print(f'Temp: {temp * 0.0625:.2f}°C')
```
evals.expected_output: Analysis of: (1) I2C bus 1 on RPi — correct for GPIO pins 2/3; (2) Address 0x48 — identifies this as likely TMP102/LM75/ADS1115 compatible sensor; (3) 12-bit ADC conversion math — the bit shift is correct for a 12-bit right-justified value; (4) No error handling for SMBus exceptions (bus not available, device not found); (5) No cleanup/close of bus handle.

assertions.text: Correctly interprets the 12-bit ADC conversion (left shift 4, right shift 4)
Identifies missing exception handling for SMBus errors
Identifies the sensor address 0x48 as a common I2C ADC/temp sensor family
